exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Squat Snatch
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in one hand at your shins, arm straight, the other hand for balance.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbell straight up overhead, arm extended.

3.Maintaining the dumbbell straight overhead, stand upright, legs extended.

4.Quickly lower the dumbbell at shoulder height, then return to starting position. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Standing Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a dumbbell on one hand at shoulder height, fists outwards, the other arm extended for counter balance. 

2.Press the dumbbell overhead, extending your arms fully. Make sure to keep your back flat and remain upright throughout. 

3.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Tricep Kickback
exercises.description:
1) Place your left knee on a flat bench with your left hand resting at the edge of the bench for balance. 

2) Start with the dumbbell hanging in your right hand, with your elbow lined up at shoulder height close to the side of your body. 

3) Press the weight back until your arm is straight and parallel to the ground. 

4) Pause, and return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.description:
1) Start by grasping a dumbbell in your left hand and standing on the edge of an elevated platform. Let your left heel drop as far as possible. 

2) Keeping your body straight, raise your left heel up as far as possible. 

3) Pause and squeeze the calf muscle, and then slowly lower your heel back down as far as possible. 

4) Repeat for desired reps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Leg Deadlift
exercises.description:
1)  When performing this exercise, ensure you keep a safe back by keeping your head and chest up and your back in a neutral or slightly arched position.  

2) Stand on one leg with the dumbbell on the same side as your standing leg. 

3) Perform the deadlift.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Leg Deadlift
exercises.description:
Stand on one leg, holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand. 

Hinge at your hips, lowering the weight towards the ground while extending the free leg behind you. 

Return to standing and repeat on the other leg.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Leg Deadlift to Row
exercises.description: Stand on one leg. Using dumbbells, hinge to lower the weights then row the weights toward your ribs. After performing a row, stand tall.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Leg Deadlift to Single Arm Row
exercises.description: Stand on one leg with the dumbbell in the hand opposite to your standing leg. Hinge to lower the weight then row the weight toward your ribs. After performing a row, stand tall.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Leg Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding a dumbbell in one hand, arm extended on the side.

2.Squat down the leg on the same side holding the dumbbell, hold the other leg off the floor.

3.Hold the bottom position for a second and then stand up, returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Preacher Curl
exercises.description:
1.Kneel on the floor with your chest supported by a preacher bench, holding dumbbells underhand and arms resting on the pad.

2.Raise the dumbbells up to shoulder height, bending at the elbows. Keep your shoulders steady as your raise the dumbbells.

3.Lower the dumbbells returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single-Leg RDL to Row
exercises.alternateName: Dumbbell Single-Leg deadlift to bent over Row
exercises.description: Holding a dumbbell in each hand, lift one leg straight behind you and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat. From this position, pull the dumbbells up toward your ribs to perform a row.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Skater Squat
exercises.description:
Hold a dumbbell in each hand. 

Perform a single-leg squat, keeping your core engaged. 

Extend the non-working leg behind you, focusing on balance and stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Snatch
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in each hand at your shin with your arms straight.

2.Maintaining squat position, raise the dumbbells up, arms extended straight overhead.

3.Keeping the arms extended overhead, stand upright.

4.Bring the dumbbells down at shoulder level then return to starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Snatch to Overhead Reverse Lunge
exercises.alternateName: Single Arm Dumbbell Snatch to Overhead Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Hold a dumbbell in one arm, snatch the weight overhead in one motion, then step back into a reverse lunge.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Split Squat
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides. Step one leg back into a split squat position, bending both knees to roughly 90 degrees, with your front knee above the ankle and your back knee pointing toward the floor. 

Keep your torso upright and core engaged. Push through your front heel to return to standing. Repeat for reps before switching legs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Split Squat Isometric Hold
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides. Step one leg back into a split squat position, bending both knees to roughly 90 degrees, with your front knee above the ankle and your back knee pointing toward the floor. 

Keep your torso upright and core engaged. Hold this position statically, maintaining tension in your legs, glutes, and core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Split Squat Pulses
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides. Step one leg back into a split squat position, bending both knees to roughly 90 degrees, with your front knee above the ankle and your back knee pointing toward the floor. 

Keep your torso upright and core engaged. From this split squat position, perform small pulsing movements by moving only a few inches up and down while maintaining tension in your legs and glutes.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Split Squat to Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
Begin in a split stance with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended at your sides, and your torso upright. 

Lower into a split squat, keeping your front knee aligned over your ankle and your back knee hovering just above the floor. As you push back up to the starting position, perform a bicep curl, bringing the dumbbells toward your shoulders. 

Lower the weights back down under control before starting the next rep.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging down both sides.

2.With your back straight, descend down until your thighs are parallel to the floor, making sure toes are pointed slightly outward.

3.Slowly return to starting position. 

4. Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat and Curl
exercises.description:
1) With this exercise, you’ll simultaneously perform a bicep curl with a squat. 

2) When you’re doing your squat, maintain a slightly arched back. 

3) Make sure you set your weight back into your heels. Keep your knees behind your toes.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat Clean
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in each hand at your shins with your arms straight.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbells straight up, catch them at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees.

3.Maintaining the dumbbells at shoulder height, stand upright, legs extended.

4.Return to starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat Clean and Jerk
exercises.alternateName: DB Clean, squat and jerk press
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding dumbbells at your shins with your arms straight.

2.ln one motion rapidly raise the dumbbells to your shoulder level.

3.Rapidly extend your hips and knees, pulling the dumbbells up, keeping your arms straight and shrugging your shoulders. Drop down as the dumbbells travel up and absorb them in a low squat

4.Then stand upright with the dumbbells at shoulder height. Then return to starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat Clean and Press
exercises.alternateName: DB clean, squat and shoulder press
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in each hand at your shins with your arms straight.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbells straight up, catch them at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees.

3.Quickly push the dumbbells overhead, extending your arms and legs fully.

4.Bring the dumbbells to shoulder height, then return to starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat Snatch
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a dumbbell in each hand at your shins with your arms straight.

2.Thrust your hips forward and pull the dumbbells straight up overhead, arms extended.

3.Maintaining the dumbbells straight overhead, stand upright, legs extended.

4.Quickly lower the dumbbells at shoulder height, then return to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat to Alternating Front Raise
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing your thighs. Lower into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest upright and core engaged. 

As you push through your heels to return to standing, lift one dumbbell at a time straight in front of you to shoulder height, alternating arms with each rep.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat to Curl to Press
exercises.alternateName: Hack squat bicep curl to shoulder press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, squat down and stand back up, then curl the weights to your shoulders, then press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat to Front Raise
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing your thighs. 

Lower into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest upright and core engaged. 

As you push through your heels to return to standing, lift the dumbbells straight in front of you to shoulder height with arms fully extended.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat to Press
exercises.description: Using dumbbells, squat down and stand back up, then press the weights overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Alternating Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding the dumbbells at shoulder height with your elbows bent and your palms facing forward.

2.Press one dumbbell overhead, extending your arm fully.

3.Lower this dumbbell and then press the other overhead, keeping your back flat throughout

4.Perform one rep on one side, then switch to the other side. Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Around the World Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart. 

Hold a dumbbell in each hand by your sides. Slowly lift both arms in a wide circular motion to meet overhead, then lower back down. 

Engage your shoulders throughout.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Back Fly
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure that you maintain a flat or slightly arched back. 

2) Try to keep your body parallel to the ground. 

3) Have the dumbbells in each hand. 

4) Move the weights out and away from each other in an arc motion. 

5) The arms should be elevated until they are parallel to the floor. 

6) Slowly lower the weights back down 

7) Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Clamshell
exercises.description:
Stand tall next to a bench or sturdy surface and hold onto it for support. Place a dumbbell on the outer thigh of the working leg for resistance. 

Shift your weight slightly onto the opposite leg. Keeping your torso upright and core engaged, lift the working leg out to the side by opening your hip like a clamshell, keeping the knee slightly bent. 

Slowly return the leg to the starting position. Maintain control and avoid leaning your torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Push Press
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, have your feet close together, knees and hips slightly bent, chest up.

2. Take a dumbbell in each hand. Raise the dumbbells to head height, the elbows out and about 90 degrees. This will be your starting position.

3.  Maintaining strict technique with no leg drive or leaning back, extend through the elbow to raise the weights together directly above your head; squeezing biceps towards the ears. 

4. Pause, and slowly return the weight to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Shoulder External Rotations
exercises.description:
Stand holding dumbbells with elbows bent at 90 degrees and tucked to your sides. 

Rotate forearms outward while keeping elbows close to your torso. Slowly return to start.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Shoulder Press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding dumbbells at shoulder height, fists outwards.

2.Press the dumbbells overhead, extending your arms fully. Make sure to keep your back flat and remain upright throughout.

3.Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Stationary Lunge
exercises.description:
1)  While performing the lunge, ensure your body is over top of your back knee.

2) Keep all your lower body segments at 90 degrees. 

3) Extend and press up. 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Step Up
exercises.description:
1. When performing the step up, ensure that your leg gets the full extention at the top of the step. Keep your knee in line with your foot and your hip as you extend your leg.

2. Stand up straight with dumbbells on each hand.

3. Place the right foot on the elevated platform. And let the left foot stay on the ground. This will be your starting position.

4. Lift your left foot all the way forward while keeping your knee slightly bent. And your right foot straight.

5. Then back to starting position.

6. Once you’re completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Step-Up to Curl Arnold Press
exercises.description:
Stand facing a bench or elevated platform, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Step one foot onto the bench, driving through your heel to lift your body upward. 

As you reach the top, perform a bicep curl, then rotate your wrists outward while pressing the dumbbells overhead. 

Rotate your palms inward as you lower the dumbbells back down to your sides. Step down with control and repeat.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Step-Up to Curl Press
exercises.description:
Stand facing a bench or elevated platform, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Step one foot onto the bench, pushing through the heel to lift your body upward. 

As you reach the top, perform a bicep curl with the dumbbells, then immediately press them overhead. Lower the dumbbells back to your sides as you step back down and repeat continuously, alternating leading legs if desired.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Step-Up to Curl Press (Alternating)
exercises.description:
Stand facing a bench or elevated platform, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Step one foot onto the bench, driving through the heel to lift your body upward. 

As you reach the top, perform a bicep curl followed by an overhead press. 

Lower the dumbbells back to your sides as you step down, then repeat on the opposite leg, alternating sides with each rep.
